title: Crotalaria magaliesbergensis
intro: Crotalaria magaliesbergensis is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as a legume or pea tree. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, as well as fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Crotalaria magaliesbergensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crotalaria magaliesbergensis is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Crotalaria magaliesbergensis is a species of plant in the legume family Fabaceae. This genus is part of the tribe Crotalarieae. The tribe Crotalarieae is one of the several tribes in the subfamily Faboideae. The subfamily Faboideae is one of the three subfamilies in the family Fabaceae, the other two being Caesalpinioideae and Detarioideae. The family Fabaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.

constituents: Crotalaria magaliesbergensis contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which are known for their hepatotoxic properties and are often found in various species of the Crotalaria genus. These alkaloids can cause liver damage and have been linked to cases of liver disease in humans and animals. The exact composition and concentration of pyrrolizidine alkaloids in Crotalaria magaliesbergensis may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growth conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents of this plant and their potential effects on human health. Crotalaria magaliesbergensis should be handled with caution, as its pyrrolizidine alkaloids can be toxic.
